projects.title: Maria
projects.righttext: El Norte es una región del mundo que se caracteriza por su clima, cultura y gente.
overviewtext: ânfora is a family-run olive grove in trás-os-montes producing small, seasonal batches of extra virgin oil. the brief was simple to state and hard to solve: give a product with no prior identity a mark that could sit on a shelf next to producers three generations older, without pretending to a history it doesn't have.

the name comes from the clay amphorae still used on the property to store oil before bottling — a detail the family mentioned almost in passing, and the one thing in the brief that felt specific rather than generic. the identity works outward from that single object: its proportion, its neck, the way light sits on unglazed clay.
the system had to hold across a hand-numbered label, a six-bottle case, a market tent, and a single social tile — so every mark was built to survive being reduced to one colour and one line weight.
projects.comment: the family didn't want a brand that looked older than it is — they wanted one that looked like it wasn't trying.

about.rightText: André Sousa is a designer working across identity, editorial, and visual systems — this page covers process, capabilities, and how to get in touch.

aboutmaintext.blocktext: André Sousa is an independent designer based between studio and screen, working on identity systems, editorial design, and the visual language that holds a brand together. the practice favors clear structure over decoration — systems that stay legible as they scale, and details that hold up under scrutiny. past projects span branding and naming, wayfinding and signage, material and texture studies, and product design. each one starts the same way: understanding what a thing needs to say before deciding how it should look.based in lisbon, working with studios and founders worldwide.
founder & creative director — andré sousa studio, 2019–present. leading brand identity and editorial projects for clients across europe.
senior designer — atelier norte, 2015–2019. developed wayfinding, signage, and packaging systems for hospitality and retail clients.

explorequotes: each work is an opportunity to explore ideas freely, while learning new skills, techniques, and ways of thinking, embodying a spontaneous and open-ended process.
